page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs running on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between single-threaded and multi-threaded execution. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25%, with single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core performance is more robust, achieving average tier results across Geekbench, stress-ng, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth also performs within the average range, reaching 90.15 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 50.79 GB/sec for uncached writes.

Given its hardware profile, the ecs.g5.6xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that can leverage its 24 dedicated cores in parallel rather than relying on high single-thread throughput. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, Redis caching deployments processing up to 16.81 million SET operations per second, and moderate database operations. It is less suitable for single-threaded legacy applications or workloads requiring high-speed local NVMe storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but provides an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU capabilities and single-threaded compression tasks are weak, placing in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. However, multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform consistently in the average tier. This resource distribution makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that do not require high single-thread performance or local SSD storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, offering average multi-core performance but weaker single-core speeds.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, delivering balanced multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance offering twenty-four dedicated cores and ninety-six gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Weaker single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Suitable for multi-threaded applications, Redis, and web serving
